record_identity.title: Algorithmic identity match converted into coercive public authority

summary: A probabilistic biometric or algorithmic identity match is treated as sufficient authority for arrest, detention, or comparable coercive state action without reliable independent verification and timely contestability.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: Identity classification becomes materially dangerous when an uncertain match is promoted into public authority. CAM must distinguish recognition evidence from identity determination and require proportionate verification before liberty-affecting action.

primary_artefact_access.access_method: Direct text review of the public AI Incident Database incident record

Incident inclusion supports pattern analysis but does not determine legal liability or final factual truth.

source_records.interpretive_reliance: Relied on as a structured secondary incident record for mechanism-level corroboration, not as an independent adjudication of every reported fact.

system_context.system_type: biometric identity and law-enforcement decision support
vendor_cluster: multiple law-enforcement agencies and biometric vendors
primary_evidenced_vendors: multiple law-enforcement agencies and biometric vendors
system_context.embodiment_status: digital system with real-world institutional effects
system_context.deployment_context: identity matching used in coercive public-authority decisions
system_context.user_role: affected person, operator, or institutional decision-maker
system_context.affected_population: people exposed to biometric identification and policing

jurisdictional_context.cross_border_relevance: high because biometric identification products and practices are deployed internationally
jurisdictional_context.public_interest_relevance: very high — liberty, due process, equality, and public authority

failure_mode_definition: An uncertain algorithmic identity output is promoted from an investigative lead into a determinative or practically determinative basis for coercive public action without independent corroboration, disclosed uncertainty, meaningful human review, and timely contestability.
failure_threshold: Met when a probabilistic identity match materially contributes to arrest, detention, charging, or an equivalent liberty-affecting intervention and adequate independent verification or redress is absent or ineffective.

failure_classification.recurrence_pattern: multiple reported incidents across deployments or organisations
failure_classification.taxonomy_reference: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 Appendix B — Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy
failure_classification.persistence: persists while probabilistic outputs can trigger consequential authority without effective independent verification, appeal, or bounded execution
failure_classification.reproducibility: mechanism corroborated across the cited incident records; individual facts remain source-bounded
failure_classification.visibility: often visible only after coercive action; uncertainty and vendor workflow may remain opaque

triage.recommended_next_step: Crosswalk current Identity and Operations doctrine for separation of recognition evidence from authoritative identity determination, independent corroboration, decision logging, appeal, and rapid correction.

affected_instruments: OPERATIONS failure taxonomy and public-authority controls
cam_internal.governance_layer: identity evidence / classification / consequential public authority / contestability
cam_internal.proposal_needed: to be assessed after corpus review

cam_internal.validator_or_automation_impact: potential validation of authority boundaries, evidence logging, and appeal prerequisites

ecosystem_status.basis: Multiple public incident records evidence the mechanism or materially similar variants.

repair_status.monitoring_status: active incident monitoring and corpus crosswalk pending

corpus_coverage.coverage_summary: The record has been classified and routed, but a deep current-corpus review is required before claiming direct, partial, or absent CAM coverage.
remaining_gaps: Review current Identity, Security, Operations, Continuity, Observability, and applicable sectoral doctrine against this mechanism.
Do not create PATCH-0025 without the separate Caelestis review-document deep dive requested by the Human Governance Editor.
